items.problem: (CheckDrift) Drift exemption unconditionally skips `local/<model>` slugs so `atcr models check` returns green even when Ollama/llama.cpp is down, the model was never pulled, or `base_url` is unreachable — a broken local setup looks healthy until the runtime request fails

liam persona binds `local/llama3.3-70b` for a "64 GB+ heavyweight" tier but docs give no pre-flight VRAM/RAM check — attempting on insufficient hardware will OOM-kill the Ollama server mid-review and abort the whole panel with no per-persona isolation

Docs give no timeout guidance for local personas — a 70B Llama on consumer hardware routinely takes 30–120s per completion; if the LLM client has a cloud-tuned default timeout (common in OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs) every local review aborts mid-stream

Model-id footgun — persona binds `local/gemma3-27b` but Ollama rejects that string (it answers to `gemma3:27b` or `gemma3-27b`); the docs explain the rewrite but a direct copy-paste of the persona `model` field into `registry.yaml` will 404 at runtime with no clear remediation

Fixture embeds the literal `sk-live-EXAMPLE-not-a-real-key` — the `sk-live-` prefix matches Stripe live-key patterns and the string ships in the binary via `//go:embed`; CI secret scanners (TruffleHog/Gitleaks rule sets that prefix-match) can intermittently fail the build or flag a committed test artifact as a real leak
Example evidence uses / as string delimiters, colliding with the / escape rule for literal

items.problem: New roster entries use `Tier: "local"` — if any downstream code switches on tier (selection logic, UI grouping, install defaults) it may have an unhandled case and silently demote or skip these personas
`gerald` is listed as `local/gemma3-27b` in the table but the prompt refers to "Gemma dense model"
